title: Plugin Browse and Autocomplete
description: Add marketplace browsing and smart /plugin argument autocomplete in the Pi TUI.

phases.name: Marketplace browse data and output
phases.description: Expose marketplace plugin listings in a reusable way and make them visible outside the TUI.

tasks.title: Add marketplace plugin listing helpers
tasks.description: Add reusable marketplace listing support around `src/marketplace.ts` and existing state records. It must return marketplace entries with stable install specs, descriptions, versions, categories, and keywords where present.
acceptance_criteria: Can list plugins for a named marketplace from added marketplace state.
Can list plugins across all added marketplaces when no marketplace is specified.
Each listed plugin exposes a display spec in `<plugin>@<marketplace>` form, and only round-tripping specs expose an actionable install value.
A generated spec is installable only when parsing it returns the original plugin and marketplace names exactly.
Non-round-tripping specs include a clear non-installable reason and are excluded from install/autocomplete values.
Missing marketplace names produce the existing style of user-facing error.
Manifest read or parse failures are returned as diagnostics so browse output and autocomplete can handle them consistently.
tasks.context: Browse and autocomplete both need marketplace plugin data without duplicating manifest parsing logic.

tasks.title: Render browse output for non-TUI use
tasks.description: Add markdown/plain formatting helpers in `src/format.ts` for marketplace plugin listings. Output must be deterministic and readable for large marketplaces without depending on command routing.
acceptance_criteria: Formatter output groups plugins by marketplace when more than one marketplace is shown.
Formatter output sorts plugins deterministically by marketplace and plugin name.
Each plugin row includes enough information to identify and install it.
Empty marketplace and no-marketplace inputs produce helpful messages that point to `/plugin marketplace add <source>`.
Per-marketplace diagnostics from listing helpers are displayed while successful marketplace listings still render.
Large listings include total counts and stable ordering so output remains scannable.
tasks.context: Non-interactive command output should be testable before command routing and should not rely on TUI APIs.

phases.name: TUI browse flow
phases.description: Add interactive marketplace and plugin selection for first-time users.

tasks.title: Add browse commands and aliases
tasks.description: Extend `src/commands.ts` so `/plugin browse [marketplace]` and `/plugin marketplace browse [marketplace]` route to the browse flow while preserving all existing command behavior and aliases.
acceptance_criteria: `/plugin browse` is accepted as a top-level command.
`/plugin browse <marketplace>` is accepted for a specific marketplace.
`/plugin marketplace browse` and `browse` aliases work from the marketplace command group.
In non-TUI mode, the browse commands print formatted browse output from P1-T002.
`/plugin help` includes `/plugin browse` and `/plugin marketplace browse` in the command reference.
Existing marketplace list/add/update/remove commands continue to work.
tasks.context: Browse should be discoverable from the same `/plugin` surface instead of introducing another top-level command.

tasks.title: Implement interactive plugin selection
tasks.description: When `ctx.hasUI` is true, use existing `ctx.ui.select` and `ctx.ui.input` primitives to choose a marketplace when needed, collect an optional one-shot search filter, and choose from a bounded plugin result list.
acceptance_criteria: Zero marketplaces shows a helpful add-marketplace message without throwing.
One marketplace opens its plugin list without an unnecessary marketplace picker.
Multiple marketplaces let the user choose a marketplace first.
When the unfiltered plugin count exceeds `PLUGIN_BROWSE_SELECT_LIMIT = 50`, the flow asks for a filter before showing plugin options.
An explicit `/plugin browse <marketplace>` skips marketplace selection and shows plugins for that marketplace.
A selected or explicit marketplace with no plugins shows a helpful message and exits without throwing or changing state.
Marketplace manifest diagnostics are shown as helpful messages instead of crashing the browse flow.
Plugin filtering matches name, description, category, or keywords and handles no-result states clearly.
Filtering is one-shot: optional text input narrows the following plugin select, with a clear no-result message and cancel path.
If filtered results still exceed the threshold, the select list is capped at 50 and the message shows the total count plus guidance to run browse again with a narrower filter.
Cancelled marketplace, filter, or plugin selections exit without state changes.
tasks.context: This mirrors the marketplace browsing expectation from Claude Code and avoids requiring users to know plugin names upfront.

tasks.title: Add install action after plugin selection
tasks.description: After a plugin is selected in the TUI browse flow, offer user-scope install, project-scope install, and cancel actions. The install actions must reuse existing install behavior and reload recommendation flow.
acceptance_criteria: Selecting user-scope install installs the selected `<plugin>@<marketplace>` for user scope.
Selecting project-scope install installs the selected `<plugin>@<marketplace>` for project scope.
Selecting cancel exits without state changes.
Ambiguous/non-installable plugin entries cannot trigger install actions and explain why they cannot be installed.
Installed-plugin output and reload recommendation match the existing `/plugin install` behavior.
tasks.context: Browse should complete the discovery-to-install loop without duplicating installer semantics.

tasks.title: Expose browse from the default plugin menu
tasks.description: Update the no-argument `/plugin` TUI menu to include a browse option and make the menu labels clear for first-time users. The menu must still expose current common actions.
acceptance_criteria: No-argument `/plugin` in TUI includes a browse marketplace/plugins option.
Existing menu actions for list, config, marketplace list/add, install, update, and help remain available.
Choosing browse from the menu starts the same browse flow as `/plugin browse`.
When no marketplaces exist, the browse menu path points users toward adding one first.
tasks.context: First-time users are most likely to run `/plugin` or discover it from slash autocomplete, so browsing should be visible immediately.

phases.name: Autocomplete
phases.description: Add smart argument completions for command discovery and common plugin operations.

tasks.title: Add autocomplete helper contract
tasks.description: Create a testable autocomplete module used by `src/index.ts`. It must accept Pi’s whole `argumentPrefix` string, return whole-argument replacement values, and isolate local-state reads from command registration.
acceptance_criteria: `src/index.ts` registers `/plugin` completions through `getArgumentCompletions`.
Completion logic is available from an exported helper or module that tests can invoke without interactive TUI internals.
The helper accepts the raw whole `argumentPrefix` string passed by Pi after `/plugin`, without cursor-position metadata.
The helper defines or imports shared constants for `PLUGIN_BROWSE_SELECT_LIMIT = 50` and `PLUGIN_AUTOCOMPLETE_LIMIT = 50`.
Completions filter by the current token prefix and return Pi-compatible autocomplete items with value, label, and optional description.
Returned item values are whole-argument replacements because Pi replaces the full `argumentPrefix`, not just the current token.
Nested completions preserve prior tokens, e.g. completing `marketplace br` returns a value like `marketplace browse`.
Tokenization follows existing `/plugin` argument parsing behavior; unsupported quote/cursor edge cases safely return no custom completions.
The helper performs only local state and manifest reads and tolerates unreadable marketplace manifests by omitting those entries.
Marketplace manifest data used for autocomplete is cached in memory and invalidated when marketplace state changes.
Representative helper tests cover empty input, partial subcommands, nested subcommands, whole-prefix replacement values, and safe fallback on errors.
tasks.context: Autocomplete is a runtime UI hook, but smoke verification needs a direct non-interactive surface.

tasks.title: Add command and config autocomplete
tasks.description: Provide autocomplete suggestions for top-level commands, marketplace subcommands, config subcommands, config keys, boolean values, and valid command flags using the helper from `src/autocomplete.ts`.
acceptance_criteria: Typing `/plugin ` suggests top-level commands including `browse`.
Typing `/plugin marketplace ` suggests marketplace subcommands including `browse`.
Typing `/plugin config set ` suggests supported config keys.
Config key and value completions are sourced from the same authoritative config metadata used by `/plugin config`.
Supported config keys, value types, and value completions are centralized in a shared metadata export consumed by config handling and autocomplete.
Typing after `claudeReadOnlyImports` suggests boolean values.
`--project` is suggested only for install and uninstall contexts where valid.
`--all` is suggested only for uninstall contexts.
Representative tests cover config subcommands, config keys, boolean values, and whole-argument replacement values.
tasks.context: Autocomplete is the primary in-TUI discovery mechanism after the top-level slash command appears.

tasks.title: Add marketplace and plugin name autocomplete
tasks.description: Autocomplete known marketplace names and plugin specs from current state. Install should suggest available marketplace plugins; enable, disable, update, and uninstall should suggest installed plugin specs.
acceptance_criteria: Marketplace update/remove/browse completions include added marketplace names.
Install completions include available `<plugin>@<marketplace>` specs from added marketplaces.
Autocomplete results are prefix-filtered before deterministic ranking and capped to `PLUGIN_AUTOCOMPLETE_LIMIT = 50`.
Completion ordering is deterministic, prioritizing exact/prefix matches and then marketplace/plugin name sort order.
Enable/disable/update/uninstall completions include installed plugin specs.
Non-round-tripping/non-installable plugin specs are excluded from install, enable, disable, update, and uninstall completions.
Autocomplete does not perform network fetches or marketplace updates.
Marketplace autocomplete uses cached local manifest data and does not reread every manifest on every keystroke when cache entries are valid.
Unreadable marketplace manifests are omitted from completions using the shared listing-helper diagnostics policy.
A generated large-manifest fixture or equivalent check confirms completions remain bounded to local parsing and filtering work.
Representative tests cover marketplace-name completions, install specs, installed plugin specs, caps, ranking, and whole-argument replacement values.
tasks.context: Users should not have to remember exact marketplace or plugin identifiers, especially with large marketplaces.

phases.name: Docs and verification
phases.description: Document the user-facing discovery workflow and verify the feature end to end.

tasks.title: Update documentation for browse and autocomplete
tasks.description: Update `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, and `/plugin help` output with the browse workflow, autocomplete discovery behavior, examples, and the difference between browsing added marketplaces and adding new marketplace sources.
acceptance_criteria: README documents `/plugin browse` and `/plugin marketplace browse`.
`/plugin help` output documents browse commands and the first-time browse/add-marketplace path.
README explains Tab autocomplete for `/plugin` arguments and plugin specs.
CHANGELOG includes an Unreleased entry describing browse and autocomplete.
Docs do not imply unsupported remote marketplace search before a marketplace is added.
tasks.context: The user asked specifically how first-time users learn available commands in the TUI.

tasks.title: Expand smoke verification
tasks.description: Extend smoke or focused verification to cover non-TUI browse output, browse alias behavior, representative autocomplete calls, and mocked interactive browse behavior against fixture marketplace and installed plugin state.
acceptance_criteria: Smoke verification covers `/plugin browse` for the fixture marketplace.
Smoke verification covers `/plugin marketplace browse` alias behavior.
Verification covers `/plugin help` mentioning browse commands.
Smoke verification exercises autocomplete for top-level commands, marketplace names, install specs, and installed plugin specs.
Focused verification covers the interactive browse flow with mocked UI for marketplace selection, threshold filtering, cancel paths, and install action reuse.
`npm run smoke` passes with an isolated Pi agent directory.
tasks.context: Browse and autocomplete are UX features, but they still need repeatable CLI-verifiable behavior before release.
